title: Red Red

overview.description: A traditional Ghanaian black-eyed bean stew cooked with tomatoes, onions and red palm oil. Rich, comforting and packed with flavour despite its simple ingredients.

ingredients: 2 tbsp red palm oil (or vegetable oil)
method: Heat the palm oil over a medium heat.
Cook the onions for 10–12 minutes until soft and beginning to caramelise.
Add the garlic, ginger and chilli and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes and smoked paprika.
Add the black-eyed beans and vegetable stock.
Simmer gently for 20 minutes until thickened.
Lightly mash some of the beans to thicken the sauce while leaving plenty whole.
Scatter with chopped herbs before serving.

learning.did-you-know: The name 'Red Red' comes from the red colour of both the palm oil and the tomato sauce used in the dish.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How caramelising onions builds sweetness, how palm oil contributes flavour, and how partially mashing beans naturally thickens a stew.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the most economical meals in the cookbook while introducing authentic West African flavours using mostly pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Red palm oil gives the dish its distinctive flavour and colour.
Mash only a few beans to naturally thicken the stew.
Don't rush the onions—they provide much of the sweetness.
Fried plantain is the traditional accompaniment if you can find ripe plantains.
notes.batch-cooking: Excellent for freezing in meal-sized portions.
notes.leftovers: Often tastes even better the following day.
